managerActions: Pull the top unread inbound queues first. Those are the cleanest manager interventions.
Coach the rep with the highest stale conversation count before pushing more new leads.
Use the Retell feed to spot recent no-connect / voicemail patterns and tighten callback handling.
Treat unanswered inbound conversations as the priority leak before more reporting vanity work.

recentQueue.lastMessageBody: Yeah I just put another one under contract today too actually

Correction: my apologies architectural plans will be complete by tomorrow. There are corrections he is working on. 


On Thu, Apr 2, 2026 at 5:42 AM Anna Mendoza <annacita322@gmail.com [annacita322@gmail.com]> wrote:

> No other refinance proceeds besides internal pro forma. 
> Permit status: architectural drawings complete. Structural engineer plans will be complete no later than a week from now. 
> I would like to point out that our project manager is a part time city of San Diego city inspector. He has connections with
> coastal commissions as well as DSD and city of oceanside. Also he knows exactly how to pass code the first time. 
> Another internal note: The RMO (responsible managing officer) of our construction company we are using is best friends with the
> president of the DSD (development services department). So he’s able to expedite plans a lot faster. He is in charge of our
> project manager. 
> Per the city by right we are allowed seven units on this lot and two more with bonus density rules. I am just doing seven. Three
> units are 3 bedroom. Three units are two bedroom. One unit is one bedroom. 
> On Thu, Apr 2, 2026 at 5:28 AM Anna Mendoza <annacita322@gmail.com [annacita322@gmail.com]> wrote:
> 
> > Pay off second lien 
> > 
> > 
> > On Thu, Apr 2, 2026 at 5:26 AM Anna Mendoza <annacita322@gmail.com [annacita322@gmail.com]> wrote:
> > 
> > > Please see attached payoff first lien. 
> > > 
> > > 
> > > On Wed, Apr 1, 2026 at 5:08 PM Morgan Chapman <morgan@futuresfundinginc.com [morgan@futuresfundinginc.com]> wrote:
> > > 
> > > 
> > > > Anna,
> > > > 
> > > > Thank you for sending the package over. We reviewed the materials and the file is much clearer now.
> > > > 
> > > > Before we can determine whether there is a workable financing structure, we need to clear up a few items:
> > > > 
> > > >  1. Please provide a full debt schedule for the property, including all current liens, notes, and payoff amounts. The
> > > >     amount owed in your email does not appear to match the payoff statement provided.
> > > >  2. Please send current permit status, including whether plans have been submitted, whether plan check is in progress, and
> > > >     any expected approval dates.
> > > >  3. Please confirm the final approved unit mix, as one of the plan sheets appears inconsistent.
> > > >  4. Please provide any third-party support for stabilized value or refinance proceeds beyond the internal pro forma.
> > > > 
> > > > Once we have those items, we can determine the most realistic path forward.
> > > > 
> > > > Best, 
> > > > 
> > > > Morgan Chapman
